summary: Acoustic gems shine, solid electric set with Scarlet>Fire
blurb: The Warfield show delivers a superb acoustic set featuring Dire Wolf, Bird Song and Ripple, followed by a tight electric second half highlighted by The Music Never Stopped and Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain. Jerry is in strong voice throughout, especially on Black Peter.
review: On October 3 1980 at San Francisco’s Warfield Theater the Dead served up an unforgettable three‑set marathon. The first set was a masterclass in acoustic storytelling – from the warm opening of Dire Wolf through the plaintive Dark Hollow and the soaring Bird Song into a beautifully sung Ripple, all wrapped in crystal‑clear sound that lets you hear every nuance of Brent’s keyboards and Jerry’s lead vocals. The electric portion never lost its momentum; Jack Straw and Sugaree kicked off a high‑energy second set that built to a highlight with The Music Never Stopped, while the third set delivered the classic Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue, a powerful Samson & Delilah, and a sprawling jam through Drums, Space and The Wheel that culminated in a soulful Black Peter before closing with Good Lovin’ and an intimate encore of Brokedown Palace. Across multiple recordings reviewers consistently note Jerry “on” during the electric stretches, especially on Black Peter, and praise the overall flow as a "PRIMO" performance – a night where acoustic intimacy meets electric fire.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three of four recordings are positive, highlighting crystal‑clear acoustic set and strong electric moments; one mixed review still praises Jerry’s playing on Black Peter. Consistent praise across sources warrants a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture of the full show with crystal‑clear audio and no reported dropouts, delivering the complete acoustic and electric experience in top fidelity.

key_highlights: Acoustic set shines with Dire Wolf, Bird Song, Ripple and China Doll.
Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue delivers classic Dead fireworks.
Strong vocal performance by Jerry, especially on Black Peter and The Music Never Stopped.
Overall flow praised as "PRIMO" – a seamless three‑set journey.

band_performance.Jerry: In strong voice throughout, leading the acoustic vibe and delivering a standout performance on Black Peter.
